perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information contained in the membership database may be used by the Receiving Party exclusively for conducting the business of the Society.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information contained in the membership database may be used by the Receiving Party for any purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the receiving party can use confidential information for 'any purpose whatsoever', this directly contradicts Section 3, which restricts the use of Confidential Information 'for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society' and prohibits using the information for their own benefit or to the detriment of the Society. This contradiction creates uncertainty about permitted uses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to use Confidential Information for 'any purpose whatsoever,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating that the Confidential Information must be used 'for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society' and not for their own benefit. This creates a clear conflict and inconsistency within the contract regarding the permitted uses of the Confidential Information, making it a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of membership, the Receiving Party is not required to return or delete any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original requirement in Section 3 that the Receiving Party must return all records and delete electronic information upon termination. It creates uncertainty about the obligations regarding confidential data after the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "the Receiving Party is not required to return or delete any Confidential Information," which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states, "Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records... Confidential Information stored electronically... will be deleted upon membership termination." This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the obligations regarding confidential data after termination.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provision of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party’s duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: The nondisclosure provision of this Agreement terminates immediately upon termination of membership.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the non-disclosure agreement terminates immediately after membership termination, this clause contradicts Section 4, which states that the non-disclosure provision shall remain in effect indefinitely. This in-text contradiction introduces significant uncertainty regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Time Period. The nondisclosure provision of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party’s duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the nondisclosure agreement survives termination indefinitely, which is also stated in the Contradicted Text. However, the changed text explicitly states the nondisclosure agreement terminates immediately upon membership termination. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, as the changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text regarding the duration of the nondisclosure provision, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: If proven Improper Disclosure sha ll take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately. Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Even if proven Improper Disclosure shall take place, the Receiving Party's membership may or may not be terminated. The Society will not take any legal action under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This statement directly contradicts Section 5. The original clause stipulates immediate termination upon Improper Disclosure and the possibility of legal action. By changing it to 'may or may not be terminated' and denying any legal action, the consequences for violating the agreement become ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Penalties. If proven Improper Disclosure sha ll take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately. Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text both state that 'If proven Improper Disclosure shall take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately' and also allows for legal recourse. The changed text modifies this by stating that membership 'may or may not be terminated' and explicitly denies any legal action, directly conflicting with the original text and contradicted text. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the consequences of Improper Disclosure, which constitutes a direct contradiction according to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: Each party has signed this Agreement through its author ized representative.
perturbation.changed_text: Only the Receiving Party is required to sign this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This contradicts the original concluding sentence, which states both parties sign the agreement. This change raises questions about the validity and enforceability of the agreement, as it suggests the Society is not bound by it, in effect it creates a contradiction because both parties are required to sign the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-disclosure Agreement and each pa rty’s obligations shall be binding on the Receiving Party and the Societ y. Each party has signed this Agreement through its author ized representative.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'Each party has signed this Agreement'. The changed text states that 'Only the Receiving Party is required to sign this agreement.' This directly contradicts the original statement, as it now implies that the Society, a party mentioned in the contradicted text ('the Receiving Party and the Society'), is not required to sign. The contradicted text also contains the phrase 'Each party has signed this Agreement through its author ized representative.', so the changed text creates a structural flaw by conflicting with the same clause existing elsewhere.
